what: block_m=64 for bs=128/E=33/d=512 with 4-WG M128 stage1 + FlyDSL t16x128x128 stage2 (instead of block_m=128)
why: block_m controls moe_sorting granularity. With 128 tokens and 33 experts (~3.9 tokens/expert avg), block_m=128 means most expert groups fit in 1 block with heavy padding. block_m=64 halves the padding waste while still having reasonable tile count for CU utilization. The 4-WG stage1 kernel (M128) handles the compute tiles independently.
